### Data Scientist at [Schlumberger](https://www.linkedin.com/company/slbglobal)
...
My experience as an intern exposed me to the energy industry’s real-world challenges of transforming data into actionable insights while understanding the intricate business workflows involved in financial planning and budget estimation. My major task involved leveraging machine Learning models and analytical based solutions to forecast the workforce, asset, and product for a planned customer and segment activity. ● Built an activity-based revenue forecasting model (using XGBoost) for scenario-based financial planning & budget estimation for new ventures by the operations team. Performed bayesian hyperparameter tuning and cross-validated with sliding-window method using mean absolute percentage error (16%). ● Performed time-series analysis with moving averages and lags to determine trends and correlations in revenue data using ADF Stationarity tests and auto-correlation plots. Presented key activity factors and data anomalies to business owners using PowerBI leading to a 5% improvement in data entry and data collection methods ● Developed quantitative models to calculate employee compliance for workforce planning using exponential smoothing methods; highlighted employee utilization metrics with customized drivers using Power-BI Show less

### Prediction of Performance Degradation in a Virtual Machine Monitor (VMM) using Machine Learning Techniques
...
Developed a system to predict performance degradation; Conducted multiple benchmark experiments to stress the VMM with various workloads; Tested the system with 5 different classifiers (Random Forests, Naïve Bayes, MLP, J48 and SVM); Chose the classifier best suited for
...
data under consideration.
...
### A survey on the application of Machine Learning algorithms to predict Software Aging
...
Prediction of software aging is of vital importance as it is considered one of the major causes of software outages. This is a survey comparing 10 papers dated between 2010 and 2012 which are based on the machine learning algorithms studied, metrics monitored, tools used, platform used for implementation, results obtained and the result validation done. This work provides researchers with the latest trends and findings of this field. This was done as a part of the literature survey needed for our undergrad thesis work: Predicting performance degradation in Virtual Machine Monitors using machine learning techniques. Show less

results.title: Syed Aaquib Hussain
highlights:
Senior Petrophysicist | Leveraging Data Science and Machine Learning expertise for innovative solutions
...
Domain Data Scientist at [SLB](https://www.linkedin.com/company/slbglobal)
...
I am excited by Schlumberger's digital future and I'm contributing to its implementation and success. My role entails providing a wide range of petrophysical and geomechanics answer products, including acoustics, nuclear, resistivity, spectroscopy, cased hole, MEM-WBS, completion quality analysis, Integrated Stress Analysis, and other advanced and integrated petrophysics-geomechanics evaluations.
...
#### Domain Data Scientist (Current)
...
As a Domain Data Scientist at SLB, I am thrilled to be at the forefront of innovation in the energy industry. In this role, I leverage my expertise in data analysis, machine learning, and domain knowledge to extract valuable insights and drive data-driven solutions. My responsibilities include:
...
- Applying advanced analytics techniques to extract actionable insights from large and complex datasets in the energy domain.
...
- Collaborating with cross-functional teams to develop and deploy predictive models, algorithms, and statistical analyses to optimize operational processes and decision-making.
...
- Conducting research and staying up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies to drive continuous improvement and innovation in data-driven solutions.
...
- Collaborating with domain experts to understand business challenges and identify opportunities for leveraging data science methodologies to solve real-world problems.
...
- Ensuring data quality, integrity, and compliance with applicable regulations and best practices. I am excited to be part of the Schlumberger team, working alongside talented professionals to unlock the full potential of data in the energy industry. Feel free to reach out if you'd like to connect, collaborate, or explore opportunities in the data science and energy space. Show less
...

results.title: Vikas Jain
highlights:
As a recognized expert and thought leader in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence, I have over 21 years of experience in the oil and gas industry, with a focus on developing and deploying intelligent data-driven workflows and solutions. I have deep expertise in subsurface evaluation and machine learning/artificial intelligence during the lifecycle of oil and gas exploration and development. My technical and business contributions have been recognized, with over 40 publications, 25 patents grants/applications, and over 270 citations. I have a strong background in project management and business development, collaborating across functions and geographies. I have also developed significant intellectual property, including over 40 publications and 25 patents grants and applications related to machine learning, data analytics, formation evaluation, geomechanics and Nuclear Magnetic Resonance. Currently, as the Artificial Intelligence Business Development Manager for Americas Land at Schlumberger, I am responsible for AI business development across the US, Canada and Latin America. I am working closely with the global AI team to build programs that will help accelerate the adoption of AI/ML, including AI Academy and professional certification. I am also identifying and alleviating the pain points, especially MLOps and governance, that delay the adoption of AI/ML at the enterprise level. I am passionate about leveraging my expertise to help organizations adopt and succeed with AI/ML technologies and I am continuously educating myself with the latest developments in the field by attending conferences, workshops and taking up additional courses. I am also regularly sharing my knowledge by delivering training sessions and presentations on AI, ML and data science.
...

As explained, log correlation tends to be performed manually by well placement engineers, which may introduce inconsistencies, latencies, etc., during drilling. As an example, a framework may implement one or more machine learning (ML) models that may automatically predict a position of a formation top during drilling, for example, responsive to receipt of data acquired by a downhole tool string (e.g., a drill string, etc.). By implementation of such a framework, well placement engineers may gain confidence in drilling operations and may be provided with time that may allow for performance of other tasks. As an example, an ML model-based approach may provide for consistency in results for drilling operations of a well or wells. As an example, an ML model-based approach may provide for continuous learning, re-training, etc., such that a framework may improve output responsive to acquisition of data (e
...
g., during a drilling job, etc.). As explained, a drilling job may include drilling of a landing section that relies on a landing point.

example, a framework may provide for achieving higher accuracy and consistency than a human-based approach, particularly, in ambiguous cases, to improve landing point determinations
...
FIG. 9 shows an example of a workflow 900 that may provide for generation of one or more ML models that may be utilized to predict a position of a formation top such as, for example, a TVD value of a formation top based at least in part on data acquired during drilling. As shown in the example of FIG. 9, the workflow 900 may include a reception block 904 for receiving an actual formation top depth (e.g., TVD of a formation top), a labeling block 908 for accessing well logs and labeling the well logs, a classification block 912 for generating classes for the well logs and their labels, a feature engineering block 916 for generating features for classes, a machine learning block 920 for predicting classes using one or more machine learning (ML) models, a statistic block 924 for computing rolling means of classes (e.g., in different windows), a summation block 928 for summing means of different windows, a prediction block 932 for predicting a formation top based on a highest value of sums of means of different windows, and an error block 936 for computing an error as a difference between a position of the actual formation top and the position of the predicted formation top (e.g., an absolute value of a difference in TVD of the actual formation top and the predicted formation top).

Another technique for machine learning uncertainty can be a determination of whether there is a high discrepancy between any of several ML models that are executed (e.g., when an ensemble of ML models are utilized in block 70 as deep ensembles). Deep ensembles are a technique used in machine learning to improve predictive performance and uncertainty estimation. They
...
using multiple models, which are trained on the same data but with randomized initial weights, to make predictions. The individual predictions from each model in the ensemble are then combined for analysis. This approach can be more computationally effective compared to other methods like Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) and Bayesian Neural Networks (BNN), and it is particularly useful for producing model uncertainty estimates when dealing with out-of-distribution data. In this manner, separate from or in addition to a data misfit analysis of the inversion data from block 70, machine learning uncertainty can be used as the criteria to decide in block 72 whether inversion results from the trained ML model in block 70 meet a predetermined criteria (or threshold).

We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
Employer VIB Location
...
Closing date 1
...
2026
...
## Job Details
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- A unique position
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

results.title: Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Joblume
highlights:
Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher | Joblume
...
# Senior–Staff Machine Learning Researcher
...
Remote, USA
...
Posted Jun 30, 2026
...
Full-time
...
## About Terra AI We are building the state-of-the-art AI platform for the discovery and development of clean energy and mineral resources. We bring the most advanced techniques in generative AI, foundation modeling, and autonomous decision optimization to tackle the most important problems in the geosciences. These systems can help more reliably identify critical resource deposits, more rapidly measure and characterize them, and design more efficient and sustainable production plans. We are backed by Khosla Ventures and other leading venture investors. We are now looking to grow our team from ~15 to ~30 by the end of the year to continue to mature our technology and support deployment with our world-class mineral and clean energy partners. Role description In the same way image generators have shown the remarkable ability to produce a diverse set of realistic pictures conditioned on a text prompt (and other inputs), we are developing a generative model that produces 3D geological models conditioned on geophysical surveys, bore hole measurements, and other forms of physical observation. The outputs of the generative model capture what we know and don’t know about the state of the subsurface, allowing explorers to make maximally informed decisions about how and where to explore for critical resources. We are looking for a talented deep learning engineer or scientist to lead the development of this model that will revolutionize decision making in the earth subsurface for a wide range of clean energy applications. Role Responsibilities Design, train, test, and iterate on diffusion models for 3D geological models Design, train, test, and iterate on an approach to for conditioning generation on geophysical data and other observations Inform the generation of synthetic data to improve model performance Adapt diffusion modeling approach to specific real-world projects in collaboration with project teams. Qualifications Required Qualifications: Extensive PyTorch Experience Deep understanding of PyTorch, including writing custom modules, optimizing training, and debugging issues in large-scale models. Expertise in Developing Large Deep Learning Models from Scratch Proven ability to design, implement, and train complex deep learning architectures from the ground up. Data Curation Skills Hands-on experience in creating, cleaning, and maintaining high-quality datasets tailored for machine learning applications. Strong Software Engineering and Design Experience Proficient in software development best practices, including version control, testing, and code optimization. Familiarity with designing scalable and maintainable systems. Bonus points if you: Experience with Generative Models Familiarity with generative architectures, particularly diffusion models, and an emphasis on posterior sampling methods. Knowledge of Transformer Architectures Experience building and training transformers, especially in applications involving 3D data. Scaling Models Across Large GPU Clusters Expertise in parallelizing models across multiple GPUs and optimizing distributed training pipelines. Cloud Infrastructure Expertise Experience setting up, managing, and optimizing cloud environments for machine learning workloads, including provisioning resources and managing costs.
